missingSurface: The SQL migration chain still lacks the widened AppointmentRecord manage-state persistence owned by 288, including non-booked appointment_status values used by reminder suppression and recomputation.
temporaryFallback: Reminder suppression and reschedule drift are implemented against the executable in-memory AppointmentRecord model already present in services/command-api and validated through reminder integration tests.
riskIfUnresolved: A database built only through the checked-in migrations cannot persist the full appointment lifecycle states that the reminder engine already enforces in code.
followUpAction: Land the 288 appointment-manage persistence migration that widens appointment_status and related manage fields, then remove the migration-gap note.
